qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Request Forgery via unvalidated URL in OnlyOffice callback handler
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes a password-protected share bypass via /public/api/share/info in backend/http/share.go (CVE-2026-30933, CWE-200/CWE-306/CWE-602). This finding describes SSRF via /api/onlyoffice/callback in backend/http/onlyOffice.go. Different vulnerable endpoint, different source file, different root cause (missing downloadURL sanitization in public share API vs unvalidated URL fetch in OnlyOffice callback), and different vulnerability class (authorization bypass/information disclosure vs SSRF).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory describes password-protected share bypass via GET /public/api/share/info in backend/http/share.go (convertToFrontendShareResponse at line 63 sets downloadURL with token; the public endpoint does not clear it). The sole qualifying finding (onlyoffice-ssrf) describes SSRF via /api/onlyoffice/callback in backend/http/onlyOffice.go — a different endpoint, different file, different root cause, and different vulnerability class.
evidence: Advisory vulnerable code: backend/http/share.go convertToFrontendShareResponse (line 63) and GET /public/api/share/info
onlyoffice-ssrf vulnerable code: backend/http/onlyOffice.go:505 http.Get(data.URL) and /api/onlyoffice/callback
No overlap in endpoint, source file, function, or root-cause mechanism
